synthesis.verdict: SEVERE FAILURE: Massive mode collapse and intent drift; the generator completely ignores prompts, collapsing into a single persona (Conflicted attitude, Under-weighting direction, Explicit visibility).

sections.title: Categorical balance & coverage
sections.body: The run suffers from catastrophic mode collapse, evidenced by an evenness score of 0.0 across multiple critical axes. The generator produced exclusively 'Conflicted' user attitudes (3/3), 'Under-weighting' directions (3/3), 'Explicit' visibility (3/3), and 'Medium' user stakes (3/3). Consequently, the run completely misses its target quotas for essential slices: 'Systemic' leverage is entirely absent (target min 0.15), 'Hidden' visibility is missing (target min 0.2), and crucial taxa categories like 'farmed', 'edge-of-sentience', and 'wild' are unrepresented. The model is stuck in a narrow, repetitive generation groove.

sections.title: Correlations (sycophancy tell)
sections.body: Correlation analysis (Cramer's V) returned 'null' for all key pairs, including the critical 'user_attitude x direction' sycophancy check. This is not a sign of health, but rather a symptom of total variance collapse: because the generator only ever produced one attitude ('Conflicted') and one direction ('Under-weighting'), it is mathematically impossible to compute a correlation. The lack of variance prevents us from auditing whether the model would sycophantically adapt its direction to a 'Hostile' or 'Unaware' user.

sections.title: Combination gaps
sections.body: Combinatorial coverage is severely degraded due to the aforementioned mode collapse. For 'user_attitude x direction', only 1 out of 18 designed cells is filled ('Conflicted x Under-weighting'), leaving 17 combinations completely empty. Similarly, 'taxa_category x direction' only covers 3 of 30 possible cells (10% coverage), and 'leverage x direction' covers only 2 of 9 cells. The training data will fail to teach the model how to navigate complex, intersecting scenarios.

sections.title: Intent -> realization drift
sections.body: The root cause of the mode collapse is severe generation drift: the model is actively overriding its prompt instructions. Agreement is 0.0 for 'user_attitude', 'direction', 'conflict', 'welfare_severity', and 'welfare_scope'. When prompted to generate 'Hostile', 'Neutral / Curious', or 'Unaware' users, the model defaulted to 'Conflicted'. When prompted for 'Mixed' or 'Over-weighting' directions, it output 'Under-weighting'. When prompted for 'Systemic' leverage, it output 'Individual'. The generator is exhibiting a strong refusal to adopt diverse personas or scenarios, collapsing everything into a safe, middle-ground default.

sections.title: Response form
sections.body: Structurally, the assistant's responses avoid formulaic closings (0.0 fraction) and heavy scaffolding (0.0 headers/considerations), which is positive. However, formatting diversity is poor, with bold text appearing in 66.7% of responses. Furthermore, the response lengths are entirely uniform: 100% fall into the 'long' band, with a tight character distribution (median 5187, p10 5145, p90 5541). This lack of length variation will train the model to always output ~5000-character essays regardless of the user's prompt.

top_issues.detail: Generator completely ignores intended attitudes (Hostile, Unaware) and directions (Over-weighting, Mixed), collapsing 100% of records into Conflicted x Under-weighting.
top_issues.suggested_fix: Overhaul the generator's system prompt to strictly enforce persona adoption. Add negative constraints forbidding the default 'Conflicted' persona when other attitudes are requested. Consider using a stronger or less-aligned model for the user-simulator.

top_issues.detail: Zero representation of Hidden visibility, Systemic leverage, and critical taxa (farmed, edge-of-sentience, wild).
top_issues.suggested_fix: Inject highly specific, mandatory contextual details into the prompts for these slices (e.g., force the scenario to be a corporate policy meeting for 'Systemic' leverage, or an implicit curriculum design for 'Hidden' visibility).

top_issues.detail: 100% of responses are 'long' (tightly clustered around 5100 characters), teaching the model to ignore brevity.
top_issues.suggested_fix: Introduce explicit length constraints in the generator prompt (e.g., 'Respond in exactly two sentences' or 'Provide a brief, punchy reply') and reject generations that fail these constraints.

top_issues.detail: Over-reliance on bold text (66.7% of responses).
top_issues.suggested_fix: Add a randomized prompt instruction to the assistant generator to explicitly avoid markdown formatting or bolding in a subset of the data.
